reasoning: The average number of protests over the 360 days before April 2025 was about 31.75 per 30-day window. March 2025 saw a drastic escalation with ~68 recorded protests linked to the rift between the Marcos and Duterte factions, particularly following an ICC arrest warrant for Rodrigo Duterte. The April 20 window captures a significant portion of this spike, making a YES highly probable. The May 13 window coincides with the final month of the campaign for the May 12 midterm elections and May Day (an annually high-protest day), keeping protests elevated above the baseline. The latter two windows (July and October) stand to see some mean reversion, though structural political polarization between Marcos and Duterte fans, along with usual large mobilizations around dates like September 21 (Martial Law anniversary), make above-average protests more likely than not. Consequently, the estimates decay gracefully toward 0.5.
